name: Sterile-neutrino dark matter
summary: A keV-scale sterile neutrino N produced via active-sterile mixing in the early universe (Dodelson-Widrow) or resonant lepton-asymmetry-driven production (Shi-Fuller). Decays N -> nu + gamma at observable X-ray energies; also acts as warm DM, free-streaming on small scales. The scenario directly connects DM to neutrino-mass physics.

addresses_puzzles.note: X-ray constraints (NuSTAR, Chandra, XMM) and Lyman-alpha constraints have squeezed the parameter space; the 3.5 keV line in galaxy clusters (Bulbul 2014) was once a candidate signal but has not been confirmed.
Same right-handed neutrinos that participate in the seesaw can serve as keV DM in the nuMSM (neutrino minimal SM).

touches_benchmarks.note: Free-streaming smears small-scale power; pure thermal sterile DM requires m > ~5.3 keV.

introduces.note: Sterile mass and mixing; production mechanism (DW vs SF) adds lepton-asymmetry parameter.

history: Dodelson-Widrow (1994) production via mixing. Shi-Fuller (1999) resonant production. nuMSM framework (Asaka-Shaposhnikov 2005) embeds keV DM in a consistent low-scale seesaw. Active in cosmology + X-ray astronomy.

references.citation: Dodelson and Widrow, 'Sterile neutrinos as dark matter', PRL 72, 17 (1994)
Asaka and Shaposhnikov, 'The nuMSM, dark matter and baryon asymmetry of the universe', Phys. Lett. B 620, 17 (2005)
